name: Toward Building Partnerships with Business Partners to Enhance Cybersecurity Across the Entire Supply Chain
owner: Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry + Japan Fair Trade Commission (METI + JFTC)
description: Issued October 28, 2022. Governs how ordering companies can request/support suppliers' cybersecurity measures without violating the Antimonopoly Act or Subcontract Act -- flags 3 risk areas: unilateral pricing, cost-burden requests, forced tool purchase.
relationToCloudSecurity: See registry/control/meti.go.jp.json in the SecID repo (CloudSecurityAlliance/SecID) for cloud/AI security relevance notes on this source.
relationToAISecurity: See the paired SecID registry entry.

license.notes: Issued directly by a national government ministry/agency as an official notification/circular -- plausibly excluded from copyright under Japan's Copyright Act Article 13(2) ("notifications, instructions, circulars, and other documents similar thereto issued by national or local government organs"), but this is a less clear-cut case than primary legislation (Art. 13(1)) and has not been legally verified. Treat as moderate-confidence permitted, not certain.

current_extraction.notes: Full text acquired (Japanese; no English version exists). jftc.go.jp was reachable via a real Playwright browser session (same technique used for the meti.go.jp-hosted documents) -- fetched via in-page fetch() and saved via base64.
